title: Emergency fix for man pages: check for broken includes
body:
Somehow the options/secret.md file generated corrupt md which
then generated corrupt .man files. Fix, and add a Makefile
check to prevent this from happening again.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
Fix broken man pages
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I tried! See commented-out Makefile stuff. It failed in the "build every commit" step, quite probably because I added the check in the wrong place. (It's running after _one_ md2man, not after _all of them are done_). It was late last night and I needed an emergency fix.

This is not what I meant, I argue we need an actual linter that checks that the markdown is valid. Your import mechanism causes it to skip things but if the markdown malformed the generated man page is likely unreadable regardless of import or not.

@ashley-cui: once the present PR merges, I will cherry-pick it on top of v4.4 in a new PR and assign it to you.
